title: Implement LICHEN-specific native BLE LCI service UUIDs
description:
Replace or gate the native LICHEN BLE UART service so combined-product images can expose native LCI under LICHEN-specific UUIDs without colliding with MeshCore NUS. Implement the UUIDs documented in docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md, preserve the current legacy mutually exclusive NUS path for CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE product images unless a separate migration Bead changes that policy, and keep RX/TX SLIP semantics unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Native LICHEN BLE LCI exposes the documented LICHEN-specific GATT service, RX/TX IPv6 SLIP characteristics, protocol version characteristic, and capabilities characteristic.
- MeshCore compatibility remains the only owner of NUS UUIDs in MeshCore product mode.
- The current legacy native NUS mode remains explicit, mutually exclusive, and tested unless a separate migration Bead updates the compatibility policy.
- RX/TX SLIP payload behavior remains compatible with the documented LCI contract.
notes:
Implemented native BLE LCI UUID profile behind CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE default, with CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE_LEGACY_NUS preserving explicit legacy NUS mode for current board-default native product images. Native profile exposes service e665960c-7c84-5606-a8d3-884507d0b7a8, RX 5e6e304a-29af-52d9-a813-306f0f888586, TX be4d4a23-876b-592b-b252-440367e18e43, version 9158dca0-14ea-5e1c-8580-b97e7c6381b8, capabilities 3d3c63f3-ce23-5451-b357-738a12c20df7. Legacy NUS mode keeps version/capability attrs absent. Board configs nrf52840dk_nrf52840, t_echo_nrf52840, and r1_neo_nrf52840 explicitly enable LORA_LICHEN_BLE_LEGACY_NUS to preserve existing native NUS behavior. Docs updated to default LICHEN-specific UUIDs and legacy NUS fallback only for mutually exclusive legacy images.

Validation on EC2 i-05e27e074db786e22 using isolated west topdir /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-ble-lci-west with clean LICHEN worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-ble-lci-validate. Twister: west twister -T lichen/tests/ble_app_owner -p native_sim --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-ble-lci-v8 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen: PASS 5/5 scenarios, 58 cases executed, 12 skipped, 0 failures/errors/warnings, 200.61s. Board builds: west build -b nrf52840dk/nrf52840 -d build-ble-nrfdk lichen/apps/gateway -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen: PASS, FLASH 194444 B/1 MB 18.54%, RAM 59504 B/256 KB 22.70%. west build -b t_echo -d build-ble-techo ...: PASS, FLASH 236228 B/1 MB 22.53%, RAM 65348 B/256 KB 24.93%, known duplicate unit-address warnings and existing hal.c return_device_if_ready unused warning. west build -b r1_neo -d build-ble-r1neo ...: PASS, FLASH 247416 B/1 MB 23.60%, RAM 73092 B/256 KB 27.88%, known duplicate unit-address and USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL warnings.

Reviews: initial three-agent review found P2 TX fragmentation test gap, P2 exact-1280 RX accept gap, and docs contradictions; fixed all. Final three-agent review after fixes reported no P0/P1/P2 findings.
close_reason: Implemented LICHEN-specific native BLE LCI UUID profile with explicit legacy NUS mode, docs updates, board legacy preservation, EC2 Twister and board-build validation, and final three-agent review with no P0/P1/P2 findings.
